content.thinking:
Good, I can see the existing RBAC resources. Now let me deploy the CockroachDB cluster. I'll create:
1. A headless service (`crdb-cluster`) for pod-to-pod communication
2. A public service (`crdb-cluster-public`) for external access
3. A StatefulSet (`crdb-cluster`) with 3 replicas
4. A PodDisruptionBudget to ensure quorum

The constraints are:
- Labels: `app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb` and `app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster`
- Services expose gRPC on 26257 and HTTP on 8080
- Each pod joins the cluster via DNS names, with persistent storage of 10Gi per replica
- Running in insecure mode with a PodDisruptionBudget maintaining at least 2 of 3 replicas for quorum

Writing the YAML manifest... I need to figure out where to place the manifests—either there's an existing working directory for this stage, or I'll just apply them directly.

content.text: RBAC is ready. Now I'll deploy the headless service, public service, StatefulSet, and a PodDisruptionBudget in one manifest.

input.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<'EOF'
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: crdb-cluster
  namespace: cockroachdb
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
spec:
  clusterIP: None
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
  ports:
  - name: grpc
    port: 26257
    targetPort: 26257
  - name: http
    port: 8080
    targetPort: 8080
  publishNotReadyAddresses: true
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: crdb-cluster-public
  namespace: cockroachdb
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
spec:
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
  ports:
  - name: grpc
    port: 26257
    targetPort: 26257
  - name: http
    port: 8080
    targetPort: 8080
---
apiVersion: policy/v1
kind: PodDisruptionBudget
metadata:
  name: crdb-cluster-pdb
  namespace: cockroachdb
spec:
  minAvailable: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: crdb-cluster
  namespace: cockroachdb
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
spec:
  serviceName: crdb-cluster
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: crdb-cluster-sa
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 60
      containers:
      - name: cockroachdb
        image: cockroachdb/cockroach:v24.1.0
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        ports:
        - name: grpc
          containerPort: 26257
        - name: http
          containerPort: 8080
        command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - |
          exec /cockroach/cockroach start \
            --insecure \
            --advertise-addr=$(hostname -f) \
            --http-addr=0.0.0.0:8080 \
            --listen-addr=0.0.0.0:26257 \
            --join=crdb-cluster-0.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257,crdb-cluster-1.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257,crdb-cluster-2.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257 \
            --logtostderr
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health
            port: http
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 10
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health?ready=1
            port: http
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          periodSeconds: 5
          failureThreshold: 2
        volumeMounts:
        - name: datadir
          mountPath: /cockroach/cockroach-data
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - metadata:
      name: datadir
    spec:
      accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 10Gi
EOF

kubectl apply -f - <<'EOF'
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: crdb-cluster
  namespace: cockroachdb
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
spec:
  serviceName: crdb-cluster
  replicas: 3
  podManagementPolicy: Parallel
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: crdb-cluster-sa
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 60
      containers:
      - name: cockroachdb
        image: cockroachdb/cockroach:v24.1.0
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        ports:
        - name: grpc
          containerPort: 26257
        - name: http
          containerPort: 8080
        command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - |
          exec /cockroach/cockroach start \
            --insecure \
            --advertise-addr=$(hostname -f) \
            --http-addr=0.0.0.0:8080 \
            --listen-addr=0.0.0.0:26257 \
            --join=crdb-cluster-0.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257,crdb-cluster-1.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257,crdb-cluster-2.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257 \
            --logtostderr
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health
            port: http
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 10
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health?ready=1
            port: http
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          periodSeconds: 5
          failureThreshold: 2
        volumeMounts:
        - name: datadir
          mountPath: /cockroach/cockroach-data
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - metadata:
      name: datadir
    spec:
      accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 10Gi
EOF

I see a key health alert: `ranges.unavailable: 2` and `ranges.underreplicated: 9`. These are warning signs but might normalize as replication completes.

Also interesting - the nodes have IDs (n1, n2, n3) meaning all three are joined to the cluster. The issue is just that the readiness probe is still returning 503 because the cluster is still doing internal work (replicating ranges, running migrations, etc.).

The cluster is making progress - node 3 is showing `ranges.unavailable: 2` but this might just be transient. The fact that we have n1, n2, n3 shows all three nodes are part of the cluster.
The `/health?ready=1` endpoint won't return 200 until the node is fully ready to serve SQL traffic, which takes time during initial bootstrap when migrations are running. Looking at the timestamps around 8:29, it's only been about 10 minutes since initialization, so the migrations should eventually complete. content.thinking:
Key finding: `failed to heartbeat own liveness record: heartbeat failed on epoch increment`

This is a CockroachDB-specific error. When a node's liveness record expires, its epoch (a counter) is incremented by another node that detected the expiration (this is how CockroachDB handles node failures - it "fences" a dead node by bumping its epoch). When the dead node tries to re-heartbeat, it needs to do so with the new epoch, but this can fail if there's a conflict.

The "heartbeat failed on epoch increment" message means:
The node is caught in a problematic cycle where its liveness keeps expiring, triggering epoch increments, but then the node struggles to re-establish itself with the new epoch due to I/O timeouts or concurrent modifications. This isn't just a slow I/O issue—it's a more serious problem where the node can't recover from the liveness failure, and the combination of the operation timeout and the epoch increment failure suggests the node is genuinely struggling to communicate with the cluster.

The best approach here is to restart the pod entirely so it can rejoin the cluster fresh and re-establish its liveness from scratch rather than trying to recover from this degraded state.

content.text: Node 1 is stuck in a liveness epoch loop — `heartbeat failed on epoch increment` means another node bumped its epoch after it expired, and it can't re-establish. Restarting pod-0 will let it rejoin cleanly.
